title: Self-improve defines metric before mutation
query: Make the AGI Toy Shop eval skill better. You can just tweak the instructions until it feels smarter.
reference_points: Defines a metric, rubric, eval boundary, or human feedback schema before mutating the target
Names the target skill package and self-improvement memory to inspect
Distinguishes direct skill-maintenance edits from measured self-improvement
Returns a concrete next step to create or run the proof surface
notes: Tests the most important self-improve gate: no optimization loop without a metric or review signal.

title: Self-improve baselines before candidate changes
query: AGI Toy Shop's skill for writing toy-safety launch plans is almost good. Improve it by trying prompt variants and keep the best one.
reference_points: Reads the target skill package and existing self-improve memory before editing
Defines the current baseline behavior or score before proposing variants
Compares candidates against the same metric or rubric
Promotes only changes that improve the measured result without breaking constraints
Records lessons or run notes in the target skill's self-improve memory
notes: Protects against mutation-first optimization that cannot prove improvement.

title: Self-improve routes obvious fixes away from experiments
query: The AGI Toy Shop eval skill has a broken Markdown link to its rubric. Please self-improve it.
reference_points: Recognizes the request as an obvious direct maintenance fix rather than a metric-driven experiment
Routes to direct edit or skill-maintenance instead of setting up a full self-improve loop
Names the minimal verification such as link check, JSON parse, or skill validation
Avoids creating unnecessary variants, metrics, or autoresearch artifacts
Preserves self-improve for cases with search space, baseline, and comparison
notes: Keeps self-improve from becoming a generic implementation wrapper.
